content.question: To what extent does SNI classification status itself operate as a sorting variable driving parental school mobility under Hungary's free-choice regime, and does this amplify segregation patterns beyond the baseline school-choice effect?
content.reduction_path: Linked administrative data study matching student-level SEN/SNI status, school enrollment transitions, and parent residential location; difference-in-differences or matching design comparing mobility patterns of SNI-classified vs. non-classified students holding baseline school choice and family characteristics constant, with regional variation in SNI prevalence as a supplementary identifying variable.
